chunk_summary: Management of severe complications of bone infections, including abscess, pyomyositis, thrombosis, and septic shock, requires imaging, surgical intervention, antibiotics, and multidisciplinary care.
learning_objectives: Understand the diagnostic and management strategies for subperiosteal abscess and pyomyositis in pediatric bone infections.
Recognize the clinical signs and management of venous thrombosis associated with osteomyelitis.
Learn the protocols for managing septic shock in severe osteoarticular infections.
Identify the importance of multidisciplinary care in complex musculoskeletal infections.
key_facts: Subperiosteal abscess often requires MRI for diagnosis and image-guided or operative drainage due to poor antibiotic penetration.
Venous thrombosis is common in femoral, pelvic, or methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus infections; Doppler evaluation and anticoagulation may be necessary.
Septic shock management includes aggressive fluid resuscitation, vasoactive support, broad-spectrum antibiotics, and urgent source control.
Repeat imaging is critical for children with worsening symptoms, especially in methicillin-resistant cases.
Multidisciplinary care involving hematology, intensive care, and orthopedic teams is essential for severe cases.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Subperiosteal abscess is a classic osteomyelitis complication and may present as a fluctuant tender swelling over bone. Infection can also break into adjacent muscle, producing pyomyositis or deep intramuscular abscess. (Indian Academy of Pediatrics Consensus Statement)
document_key_topics: Septic shock in severe musculoskeletal infections
Multidisciplinary care in pediatric bone infections
